equiml
  • metrics
  • View page source

metrics

Base class

class equiml.metrics.FairnessMetric[source]

Base class for all fairness metrics.

abstractmethod compute(y_true, y_pred, sensitive, **kwargs)[source]

Compute the fairness metric.

Parameters:

  • y_true (np.ndarray) – Ground truth labels.

  • y_pred (np.ndarray) – Predictions (discrete labels or probabilities).

  • sensitive (np.ndarray or list[np.ndarray]) – Sensitive attribute(s). Pass a single np.ndarray for a single attribute, or a list of np.ndarray for intersectional analysis.

  • **kwargs – Additional metric-specific parameters.

Returns:

Rich result object containing value, groups, CI, p-value, curve.

Return type:

MetricResult

property name: str

Metric name — defaults to lowercase class name.

abstract property requires: set[str]

Return the set of prediction types required by this metric.

Returns:

Subset of {‘labels’, ‘proba’}.

Return type:

set[str]

Examples

>>> metric.requires
{'labels'}
>>> metric.requires
{'labels', 'proba'}
class equiml.metrics.base.MetricResult(name, value, groups=<factory>, confidence_interval=None, p_value=None, curve=None, extra=<factory>)[source]

Result of a fairness metric computation.

name

Name of the metric.

Type:

str

value

Main scalar value of the metric (e.g. gap between groups).

Type:

float

groups

Per-group breakdown of the metric value.

Type:

dict[str, float]

confidence_interval

(lower, upper) confidence interval for the metric value, if computed.

Type:

tuple[float, float] | None

p_value

p-value from statistical test, if computed.

Type:

float | None

curve

Curve data (e.g. ROC curve, calibration curve), if applicable.

Type:

np.ndarray | None

extra

Any additional metric-specific information.

Type:

dict[str, Any]

Discrete metrics

class equiml.metrics.DemographicParity(statistical_test=False)[source]

Demographic Parity (Statistical Parity).

Measures the difference in positive prediction rates between groups. A value of 0 indicates perfect demographic parity. First introduced in [DP1] and further studied in [DP2].

For 2 groups:

\[\Delta_{DP} = P(\hat{Y}=1 | S=a) - P(\hat{Y}=1 | S=b)\]

For more than 2 groups, value is the max gap across all group pairs.

References

[DP1]

Dwork, C., Hardt, M., Pitassi, T., Reingold, O., & Zemel, R. (2012). Fairness through awareness. In Proceedings of the 3rd Innovations in Theoretical Computer Science Conference (pp. 214-226). https://doi.org/10.1145/2090236.2090255

[DP2]

Calders, T., & Verwer, S. (2010). Three naive Bayes approaches for discrimination-free classification. Data Mining and Knowledge Discovery, 21(2), 277-292. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10618-010-0190-x

compute(y_true, y_pred, sensitive, **kwargs)[source]

Compute the fairness metric.

Parameters:

  • y_true (np.ndarray) – Ground truth labels.

  • y_pred (np.ndarray) – Predictions (discrete labels or probabilities).

  • sensitive (np.ndarray or list[np.ndarray]) – Sensitive attribute(s). Pass a single np.ndarray for a single attribute, or a list of np.ndarray for intersectional analysis.

  • **kwargs – Additional metric-specific parameters.

Returns:

Rich result object containing value, groups, CI, p-value, curve.

Return type:

MetricResult

property name: str

Metric name — defaults to lowercase class name.

property requires: set[str]

Return the set of prediction types required by this metric.

Returns:

Subset of {‘labels’, ‘proba’}.

Return type:

set[str]

Examples

>>> metric.requires
{'labels'}
>>> metric.requires
{'labels', 'proba'}
class equiml.metrics.EqualOpportunity(statistical_test=False)[source]

Equal Opportunity.

Measures the difference in True Positive Rates (TPR) between groups. A value of 0 indicates equal opportunity. Introduced in [EO1].

For 2 groups:

\[\Delta_{EO} = TPR(S=a) - TPR(S=b)\]

For more than 2 groups, value is the max TPR gap across all group pairs.

References

[EO1]

Hardt, M., Price, E., & Srebro, N. (2016). Equality of opportunity in supervised learning. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 29, 3315-3323. https://arxiv.org/abs/1610.02413

compute(y_true, y_pred, sensitive, **kwargs)[source]

Compute the fairness metric.

Parameters:

  • y_true (np.ndarray) – Ground truth labels.

  • y_pred (np.ndarray) – Predictions (discrete labels or probabilities).

  • sensitive (np.ndarray or list[np.ndarray]) – Sensitive attribute(s). Pass a single np.ndarray for a single attribute, or a list of np.ndarray for intersectional analysis.

  • **kwargs – Additional metric-specific parameters.

Returns:

Rich result object containing value, groups, CI, p-value, curve.

Return type:

MetricResult

property name: str

Metric name — defaults to lowercase class name.

property requires: set[str]

Return the set of prediction types required by this metric.

Returns:

Subset of {‘labels’, ‘proba’}.

Return type:

set[str]

Examples

>>> metric.requires
{'labels'}
>>> metric.requires
{'labels', 'proba'}
class equiml.metrics.EqualizedOdds(statistical_test=False)[source]

Equalized Odds.

Measures differences in both TPR and FPR between groups. Returns the max of TPR gap and FPR gap. Introduced in [EOdds1].

A value of 0 indicates equalized odds.

References

[EOdds1]

Hardt, M., Price, E., & Srebro, N. (2016). Equality of opportunity in supervised learning. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 29, 3315-3323. https://arxiv.org/abs/1610.02413

compute(y_true, y_pred, sensitive, **kwargs)[source]

Compute the fairness metric.

Parameters:

  • y_true (np.ndarray) – Ground truth labels.

  • y_pred (np.ndarray) – Predictions (discrete labels or probabilities).

  • sensitive (np.ndarray or list[np.ndarray]) – Sensitive attribute(s). Pass a single np.ndarray for a single attribute, or a list of np.ndarray for intersectional analysis.

  • **kwargs – Additional metric-specific parameters.

Returns:

Rich result object containing value, groups, CI, p-value, curve.

Return type:

MetricResult

property name: str

Metric name — defaults to lowercase class name.

property requires: set[str]

Return the set of prediction types required by this metric.

Returns:

Subset of {‘labels’, ‘proba’}.

Return type:

set[str]

Examples

>>> metric.requires
{'labels'}
>>> metric.requires
{'labels', 'proba'}
class equiml.metrics.PredictiveParity(statistical_test=False)[source]

Predictive Parity.

Measures the difference in precision between groups. A value of 0 indicates predictive parity. Studied in [PP1] in the context of recidivism prediction.

For 2 groups:

\[\Delta_{PP} = Precision(S=a) - Precision(S=b)\]

For more than 2 groups, value is the max precision gap.

References

[PP1]

Chouldechova, A. (2017). Fair prediction with disparate impact: A study of bias in recidivism prediction instruments. Big Data, 5(2), 153-163. https://doi.org/10.1089/big.2016.0047

compute(y_true, y_pred, sensitive, **kwargs)[source]

Compute the fairness metric.

Parameters:

  • y_true (np.ndarray) – Ground truth labels.

  • y_pred (np.ndarray) – Predictions (discrete labels or probabilities).

  • sensitive (np.ndarray or list[np.ndarray]) – Sensitive attribute(s). Pass a single np.ndarray for a single attribute, or a list of np.ndarray for intersectional analysis.

  • **kwargs – Additional metric-specific parameters.

Returns:

Rich result object containing value, groups, CI, p-value, curve.

Return type:

MetricResult

property name: str

Metric name — defaults to lowercase class name.

property requires: set[str]

Return the set of prediction types required by this metric.

Returns:

Subset of {‘labels’, ‘proba’}.

Return type:

set[str]

Examples

>>> metric.requires
{'labels'}
>>> metric.requires
{'labels', 'proba'}

Continuous metrics

class equiml.metrics.AUCParity(statistical_test=False)[source]

AUC Parity.

Measures the difference in ROC AUC scores between groups. A value of 0 indicates perfect AUC parity. Based on the framework of [AUC1], with fairness extensions from [AUC2].

For 2 groups:

\[\Delta_{AUC} = AUC(S=a) - AUC(S=b)\]

For more than 2 groups, value is the max AUC gap across all group pairs. Per-group ROC curves are available in extra['roc_curves'].

References

[AUC1]

Hand, D.J. (2009). Measuring classifier performance: a coherent alternative to the area under the ROC curve. Machine Learning, 77(1), 103-123. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10994-009-5119-5

[AUC2]

Borkan, D., Dixon, L., Sorensen, J., Thain, N., & Vasserman, L. (2019). Nuanced metrics for measuring unintended bias with real data for text classification. In Companion Proceedings of the World Wide Web Conference (pp. 491-500). https://doi.org/10.1145/3308560.3317593

compute(y_true, y_pred, sensitive, **kwargs)[source]

Compute the fairness metric.

Parameters:

  • y_true (np.ndarray) – Ground truth labels.

  • y_pred (np.ndarray) – Predictions (discrete labels or probabilities).

  • sensitive (np.ndarray or list[np.ndarray]) – Sensitive attribute(s). Pass a single np.ndarray for a single attribute, or a list of np.ndarray for intersectional analysis.

  • **kwargs – Additional metric-specific parameters.

Returns:

Rich result object containing value, groups, CI, p-value, curve.

Return type:

MetricResult

property name: str

Metric name — defaults to lowercase class name.

property requires: set[str]

Return the set of prediction types required by this metric.

Returns:

Subset of {‘labels’, ‘proba’}.

Return type:

set[str]

Examples

>>> metric.requires
{'labels'}
>>> metric.requires
{'labels', 'proba'}
class equiml.metrics.CalibrationParity(n_bins=10, statistical_test=False)[source]

Calibration Parity.

Measures whether predicted probabilities are equally well calibrated across groups, via the Expected Calibration Error (ECE) per group. Based on the ECE metric from [CAL2], with fairness analysis from [CAL1].

A value of 0 indicates perfect calibration parity.

References

[CAL1]

Pleiss, G., Raghavan, M., Wu, F., Kleinberg, J., & Weinberger, K.Q. (2017). On fairness and calibration. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 30. https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.02012

[CAL2]

Naeini, M.P., Cooper, G., & Hauskrecht, M. (2015). Obtaining well calibrated probabilities using Bayesian binning into quantiles. In Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence (Vol. 29, No. 1).

compute(y_true, y_pred, sensitive, **kwargs)[source]

Compute the fairness metric.

Parameters:

  • y_true (np.ndarray) – Ground truth labels.

  • y_pred (np.ndarray) – Predictions (discrete labels or probabilities).

  • sensitive (np.ndarray or list[np.ndarray]) – Sensitive attribute(s). Pass a single np.ndarray for a single attribute, or a list of np.ndarray for intersectional analysis.

  • **kwargs – Additional metric-specific parameters.

Returns:

Rich result object containing value, groups, CI, p-value, curve.

Return type:

MetricResult

property name: str

Metric name — defaults to lowercase class name.

property requires: set[str]

Return the set of prediction types required by this metric.

Returns:

Subset of {‘labels’, ‘proba’}.

Return type:

set[str]

Examples

>>> metric.requires
{'labels'}
>>> metric.requires
{'labels', 'proba'}
class equiml.metrics.BrierParity(statistical_test=False)[source]

Brier Score Parity.

Measures the difference in Brier scores between groups. The Brier score is the mean squared error between predicted probabilities and true labels. Based on the Brier score [BS1], with fairness analysis from [BS2].

A value of 0 indicates perfect Brier score parity.

References

[BS1]

Brier, G.W. (1950). Verification of forecasts expressed in terms of probability. Monthly Weather Review, 78(1), 1-3. https://doi.org/10.1175/1520-0493(1950)078<0001:VOFEIT>2.0.CO;2

[BS2]

Pleiss, G., Raghavan, M., Wu, F., Kleinberg, J., & Weinberger, K.Q. (2017). On fairness and calibration. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 30. https://arxiv.org/abs/1709.02012

compute(y_true, y_pred, sensitive, **kwargs)[source]

Compute the fairness metric.

Parameters:

  • y_true (np.ndarray) – Ground truth labels.

  • y_pred (np.ndarray) – Predictions (discrete labels or probabilities).

  • sensitive (np.ndarray or list[np.ndarray]) – Sensitive attribute(s). Pass a single np.ndarray for a single attribute, or a list of np.ndarray for intersectional analysis.

  • **kwargs – Additional metric-specific parameters.

Returns:

Rich result object containing value, groups, CI, p-value, curve.

Return type:

MetricResult

property name: str

Metric name — defaults to lowercase class name.

property requires: set[str]

Return the set of prediction types required by this metric.

Returns:

Subset of {‘labels’, ‘proba’}.

Return type:

set[str]

Examples

>>> metric.requires
{'labels'}
>>> metric.requires
{'labels', 'proba'}
